title: dalbergia tinnevelliensis
intro: Dalbergia tinnevelliensis, also known as Tinnevelly rosewood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. This plant is native to India and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dalbergia tinnevelliensis include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's heartwood is often used to make medicines, and it can also be used as a wood substitute due to its durability and resistance to decay.
